mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 4.99% represents a restrictive monetary stance. Sustained elevated FEDFUNDS above 4.5% reduces equity valuations by increasing the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 4.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) at 1957k units signal robust residential construction demand. Elevated housing activity drives forward equity returns through increased capital expenditure, employment, and consumer wealth effects, producing positive SPX returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1957k units as of May 2006
failure_condition: HOUST declines by more than 15% over the subsequent 2 months without corresponding SPX positive returns, or correlation between HOUST and 60-day forward returns falls below 0.10 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate at 4.6% combined with payroll employment (PAYEMS) at 135,230k indicates tight labor market conditions. When UNRATE falls below 5.0% while PAYEMS grows month-over-month, this signals strong labor demand that supports corporate earnings growth and equity valuations, producing positive 20-day forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.6% and PAYEMS at 135,230k as of June 2006
regime_hint: tight labor market with sustained employment growth
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the joint signal (UNRATE < 5.0% AND PAYEMS growth) shows Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) at 112.1 index points reflects manufacturing capacity utilization. When INDPRO growth decelerates below 0.3% month-over-month, this signals weakening supply-side momentum and potential margin compression, producing negative SPX returns over 5 trading days as forward-looking investors reprrice earnings expectations.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances when INDPRO growth < 0.3%, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) at 363,806M dollars combined with housing starts (HOUST) at 1957k units jointly indicate robust consumer and residential demand. When both series show month-over-month growth simultaneously, this signals broad-based demand strength that supports equity valuations, producing positive 60-day forward returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0% AND HOUST month-over-month growth > 0%
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 363,806M and HOUST at 1957k as of May 2006
regime_hint: synchronized demand expansion across consumer and residential sectors
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the joint interaction signal shows Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL at 201.9 (index) combined with FEDFUNDS at 4.99% indicates the Fed is maintaining real rates in restrictive territory. When CPI growth accelerates above 0.3% month-over-month while FEDFUNDS remains above 4.5%, the Fed is expected to maintain or raise rates further, compressing equity multiples and producing negative 20-day forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 201.9 and FEDFUNDS at 4.99% as of May-June 2006
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite inflation-rate signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) at 135,230k shows the absolute level of labor market strength. When PAYEMS declines by more than 50k month-over-month, this signals deteriorating labor demand that precedes earnings downgrades and equity selloffs, producing negative 5-day forward returns as the market reprices recession risk.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 65% of instances when PAYEMS declines > 50k, or the signal shows Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) at 11,403.6B dollars represents the baseline demand level. When GDPC1 growth decelerates to below 2.0% annualized (measured quarter-over-quarter), this signals late-cycle slowdown that reduces forward earnings expectations, producing negative 60-day forward returns as investors anticipate margin compression and slower growth.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances when GDPC1 growth < 2.0%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.
